title: `open --watch` is advertised but never used
reasoning: The `open` command declares a `--watch` option, but the action handler never reads `o.watch` and never forwards any watch-related field in the `request('open', ...)` payload. Users can supply the flag without any observable effect, which is a direct mismatch between the CLI surface and actual behavior.
reproduction: Invoke `glimpse open page.html --watch`; the request payload is identical to the same command without `--watch`.
recommendation: Either implement the watch behavior end-to-end or remove the flag until support exists so the CLI does not promise a no-op feature.
whyTestsDoNotAlreadyCoverThis: There are no linked tests for command option handling, so this dead flag is not exercised anywhere in the provided evidence.
suggestedRegressionTest: Add a command test for `open --watch` that asserts either `watch: true` is sent in the IPC request or that the parser rejects/omits the option if the feature is intentionally unsupported.

history.reasoning: The original evidence still exists at src/cli.ts:25: the open command still declares .option('--watch'), but the action constructs request('open', {...}) without reading o.watch or forwarding any watch field. Current daemon handling also has no watch support: src/daemon/daemon.ts dispatches open by passing params to WindowRegistry.open, and src/daemon/window-registry.ts open() accepts only name/replace/options/source/bridge/security and contains no watcher setup. A repository search found watch-related behavior only in docs plus the unused CLI option, not in implementation or tests. bun run typecheck passes. bun test could not fully complete under the read-only sandbox because tests that call mkdtempSync fail with EPERM, but the passing tests do not cover open --watch.

The original evidence is stale. `src/cli.ts:25` still exists but is now only shared option setup; the `open --watch` command is on `src/cli.ts:29` and the handler now reads `o.watch`, rejects invalid non-file sources, computes `watchPath`, forwards `source: { kind: 'html', path: src, watch: Boolean(o.watch) }`, and includes `watchPath` in the `request('open', ...)` payload. The daemon path also uses it: `src/daemon/daemon.ts:8` passes open params to `WindowRegistry.open`, and `src/daemon/window-registry.ts:13-31` accepts `watchPath` and starts `watchHtmlFile`; `src/daemon/watchers.ts:10-36` implements fs watching and reload callbacks. `bun run typecheck` passed and `bun test test/cli-options.test.ts` passed, including a current `open --watch` validation test. Full `bun test` and `test/watchers.test.ts` could not complete in this read-only sandbox because tests that call `mkdtempSync` fail with EPERM under `/var/tmp`, but the current code and available passing tests show the original no-op CLI flag contract mismatch is fixed.
